name: metal_gpu_kernels_v1
description: adapterOS Metal GPU kernel training data: core GPU operations (GEMM, softmax, norm, LoRA), performance optimization (fusion, coalescing, profiling), quantized computation paths, and debugging/profiling workflows on Apple Silicon.

entries.notes: 1500 examples across five categories: core_operations (300), performance_optimization (300), lora_specific_kernels (300), quantized_computation (300), debugging_and_profiling (300). All examples grounded in Apple Metal + MLX framework semantics as implemented in adapterOS's adapteros-lora-mlx-ffi and adapteros-lora-worker crates.

provenance.review_notes: All Metal and MLX concepts sourced from Apple Metal Programming Guide, MLX framework documentation, and the adapterOS codebase implementation. Threadgroup sizes, memory layouts, and kernel dispatch patterns reflect real Apple Silicon GPU architecture (M1/M2/M3/M4 families). LoRA kernel examples grounded in the FFILoraAdapter and MLXFFIBackend implementations.

evaluation_gates: Metal threadgroup dimensions are valid powers of 2 or multiples of 32
Memory access patterns correctly describe coalesced vs strided access
GEMM tiling dimensions are realistic for Apple GPU SIMD width (32 threads)
Quantization bit widths are 4-bit or 8-bit as supported by MLX
Metal storage modes (Shared, Private, Managed) are used correctly for Apple Silicon unified memory
Profiling tool references match real Apple toolchain (Instruments, Metal System Trace, GPU counters)
Kernel fusion examples correctly identify which operations can be fused without intermediate materialization
intent: Teach adapters to understand how GPU kernels execute on Apple Silicon via Metal and MLX: correct threadgroup layout for each operation, memory access patterns that maximize bandwidth, LoRA-specific kernel decompositions, quantized computation trade-offs, and systematic GPU debugging and profiling workflows.

subcategories.core_operations: How each fundamental operation (GEMM, softmax, LayerNorm, RMSNorm, LoRA forward, KV cache) maps to Metal compute kernels. Threadgroup layout, shared memory usage, memory access patterns, and dispatch geometry.
subcategories.performance_optimization: Kernel fusion strategies (matmul+bias+activation, attention score+softmax), memory coalescing, bank conflict avoidance, optimal threadgroup sizes for Apple GPU architecture, and reduction patterns.
subcategories.lora_specific_kernels: Efficient low-rank multiplication decomposition (A @ (B @ x)), rank-dependent kernel selection, batched LoRA for multiple adapters, alpha scaling, and adapter hotswap implications for GPU state.
subcategories.quantized_computation: 4-bit and 8-bit matrix multiplication kernels, dequantization strategies (on-the-fly vs pre-dequantize), group quantization, mixed-precision accumulation, and accuracy vs throughput trade-offs.
subcategories.debugging_and_profiling: Metal debugger workflows, GPU profiling with Instruments and Metal System Trace, identifying compute vs memory bottlenecks, common performance pitfalls on Apple Silicon, and systematic optimization methodology.
